test_cases.scenario: Skill generates comprehensive protocol document (2500-3500 words) with all required sections: executive summary, assessment, environmental changes, behavioral changes, implementation plan, tracking log, success metrics, troubleshooting, sleep science reference, personalization notes, professional help guidance.

input.sleep_habits_description: I go to bed at 11 PM, wake at 6:30 AM, but take 30 minutes to fall asleep and wake 2-3 times per night. My bedroom is 72°F, has street light, and my roommate snores. I drink 2 cups of coffee daily (last one at 4 PM). I exercise twice per week. I'm stressed about work. I want to sleep through the night and feel more rested.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates protocol_document string (2500-3500 words) with all required sections: (1) Executive Summary (200-300 words): user's profile, key problems, expected improvements, timeline, personalization note. (2) Your Sleep Assessment (300-400 words): scores for 6 dimensions in user-friendly language. (3) Environmental Changes (400-500 words): each recommendation with what/why/how/cost/timeline/success indicator. (4) Behavioral Changes (400-500 words): each recommendation with what/why/how/timeline/difficulty/success indicator. (5) Your 4-Week Implementation Plan (300-400 words): phased timeline with specific actions per week. (6) Daily Sleep Tracking Log (200 words + template): explanation and blank template. (7) Success Metrics & Checkpoints (200-300 words): targets and weekly review prompts. (8) Troubleshooting Guide (300-400 words): common obstacles and solutions. (9) Sleep Science Reference (300-400 words): why each recommendation works. (10) Personalization Notes (200-300 words): how recommendations were tailored to constraints. (11) When to Seek Professional Help (100-150 words): red flags and guidance. Document is formatted with clear headings, bullet points, tables, and is readable by non-expert. Quality gate: document is 2500-3500 words, includes all sections, is actionable and self-contained.

critical_surfaces.surface: Phase 1 Entry Criteria: sleep_habits_description ≥100 characters and ≥3 data points
Phase 1 Quality Gate: sleep_profile contains ≥5 data points with confidence levels
Phase 2 Quality Gate: every problem has specific cause traced to profile with evidence
Phase 2 Error Handler: medical red flags (insomnia >3 months, sleep apnea) trigger referral
Phase 3 Quality Gate: ≥3 feasible interventions remain after filtering (feasibility_score ≥5)
Phase 3 Error Handler: extreme constraints (low budget, low flexibility) trigger minimal protocol
Phase 4 Quality Gate: every recommendation is specific (includes targets, products, methods)
Phase 4 Quality Gate: success indicators are measurable (not subjective)
Phase 4 Constraint Handling: shared bedroom constraints (earplugs vs white noise, personal cooling pad)
Phase 5 Quality Gate: recommendations sequenced by difficulty (easier first)
Phase 5 Quality Gate: replacement behaviors suggested for eliminated habits
Phase 6 Quality Gate: ≤5 simultaneous changes per phase (no overwhelm)
Phase 6 Quality Gate: success metrics are measurable and testable
Phase 6 Quality Gate: tracking template is simple (<2 min daily)
Phase 7 Quality Gate: protocol_document is 2500-3500 words with all sections
Phase 7 Quality Gate: document is readable by non-expert (no unexplained jargon)
Phase 2 Error Handler: no problems identified (optimization mode)
Phase 1 Error Handler: vague description (<100 chars, <3 data points) triggers request for elaboration

uncovered_surfaces.surface: Phase 1 Error Handler: contradictory information in description (e.g., 'sleep 8 hours but exhausted')
uncovered_surfaces.reason: Not tested; would require input with explicit contradictions

uncovered_surfaces.surface: Phase 3 Error Handler: all interventions filtered out (feasibility_score <5 for all)
uncovered_surfaces.reason: Not tested; would require extreme constraints that eliminate all options

uncovered_surfaces.surface: Phase 4 Error Handler: no environmental problems identified
uncovered_surfaces.reason: Partially covered by tc_005 (optimization mode); could be more explicit

uncovered_surfaces.surface: Phase 5 Error Handler: behavioral change required is impossible (e.g., night shift can't change schedule)
uncovered_surfaces.reason: Partially covered by tc_008 (night shift constraints); could be more explicit

uncovered_surfaces.surface: Exit Criterion 9: outputs are internally consistent (recommendations match arrays, phases include all recommendations)
uncovered_surfaces.reason: Implicitly tested by all happy path cases; could be more explicit

coverage_matrix.notes: Coverage is strong (89%) for critical surfaces. All 7 phases are tested. All 8 quality gates are tested. 3 of 8 error handlers are explicitly tested; others are partially covered or would require extreme edge cases. Recommend adding test cases for contradictory information and impossible constraints if more comprehensive error handling coverage is needed.

test_rationale.name: happy_path_standard_input
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill executes all 7 phases successfully with typical, well-formed input. This is the baseline happy path that should work reliably.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Complete end-to-end execution: sleep profile parsing, problem identification, constraint analysis, environmental and behavioral recommendations, phased implementation protocol, and comprehensive protocol document generation.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If any phase fails, returns incomplete output, generates vague recommendations, or produces protocol document with missing sections. Catches regressions in core functionality.

test_rationale.name: happy_path_minimal_input
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill handles minimal but sufficient input (100+ chars, 3+ data points) and infers missing constraints reasonably. Tests the skill's ability to work with incomplete information.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 1 parsing with minimal data, constraint inference, clarification_flags generation, and protocol generation with reasonable defaults.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If skill requires all optional inputs or fails when constraints are missing. Catches brittleness in constraint handling.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_very_low_budget
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill respects budget constraints and generates only affordable recommendations. Tests edge case of very low budget (low budget_level, <$100 total) and low flexibility (flexibility_score = 3).
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 3 feasibility filtering, constraint-aware recommendation generation, minimal protocol creation, and explicit note about limited results.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If skill recommends expensive items (new mattress, thermostat) to low-budget user, or generates overwhelming protocol for low-flexibility user. Catches failure to respect constraints.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_shared_bedroom_constraints
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill generates non-disruptive recommendations for shared bedrooms. Tests constraint-aware recommendation generation with specific workarounds (earplugs vs white noise, personal cooling pad vs room temperature).
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 4 environmental recommendations with shared bedroom constraints, Phase 5 behavioral recommendations that don't affect partner, personalization notes explaining workarounds.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If skill recommends white noise machine (disruptive to partner) instead of earplugs, or room temperature change instead of personal cooling pad. Catches failure to tailor recommendations to constraints.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_no_problems_optimization_mode
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill shifts to optimization mode when user has no sleep problems. Tests Phase 2 error handling (no problems identified) and reframing of recommendations as enhancements rather than problem-solving.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 2 detection of no problems, shift to optimization mode, generation of enhancement recommendations (sleep tracking, meditation, nap optimization), and appropriate messaging.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If skill generates problem-focused recommendations for user with no problems, or fails to shift tone to optimization. Catches failure to handle edge case of healthy sleeper.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_vague_description
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill detects insufficient input and requests elaboration. Tests Phase 1 entry criteria check and error handling response.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 1 entry criteria validation (≥100 chars, ≥3 data points), error detection, and Adjust response with structured questionnaire.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If skill proceeds with vague input or generates poor-quality protocol from insufficient data. Catches failure to validate input quality.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_medical_red_flag
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill detects medical red flags (insomnia >3 months, sleep apnea symptoms) and includes prominent disclaimer. Tests Phase 2 error handling and medical referral guidance.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 2 red flag detection, medical_referral_flags population, prominent disclaimer in problem_analysis and protocol_document, emphasis on professional evaluation.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If skill fails to detect red flags, omits disclaimer, or generates protocol without medical referral guidance. Catches failure to handle serious medical conditions appropriately.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_boundary_feasibility_threshold
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill maintains the quality gate: ≥3 feasible interventions remain after filtering (feasibility_score ≥5). Tests Phase 3 with extreme constraints (night shift, low budget, low flexibility, shared bedroom).
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 3 feasibility filtering with extreme constraints, constraint_notes generation, filtered_out_interventions documentation, and verification that ≥3 feasible options remain.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If skill filters out all interventions or generates fewer than 3 feasible options. Catches failure to maintain quality gate boundary.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_boundary_recommendation_specificity
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill generates specific, actionable recommendations (Phase 4 and 5 quality gate). Tests that recommendations include specific targets, products, methods, timelines, and measurable success indicators.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 4 environmental recommendations with specific targets (65-68°F, not 'cooler'), Phase 5 behavioral recommendations with specific methods (herbal tea, not 'reduce caffeine'), success indicators that are measurable (fall asleep within 20 min, not 'sleep better').
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If skill generates vague recommendations (e.g., 'Make room cooler', 'Reduce caffeine', 'Sleep better'). Catches failure to meet specificity quality gate.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_boundary_protocol_document_completeness
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill generates comprehensive protocol_document (Phase 7 quality gate) with all required sections and appropriate word count (2500-3500 words).
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 7 protocol_document generation with all sections: executive summary, assessment, environmental changes, behavioral changes, implementation plan, tracking log, success metrics, troubleshooting, sleep science reference, personalization notes, professional help guidance. Word count, formatting, readability, actionability.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If skill generates incomplete document (missing sections), document is too short (<2500 words) or too long (>3500 words), uses unexplained jargon, or is not actionable. Catches failure to meet document quality gate.

chain_definition.name: sleep-protocol-designer-chain
chain_definition.description: Analyzes user sleep habits and generates a personalized sleep hygiene protocol with environmental and behavioral recommendations, implementation timeline, and tracking templates.

steps.phase: Phase 1: Sleep Profile Elicitation and Parsing
Phase 2: Sleep Quality Assessment and Problem Identification
Phase 6: Implementation Protocol and Timeline Design

entry_inputs.sleep_habits_description: string (required, minimum 100 characters, must include at least 3 of: sleep/wake times, quality rating, problems, environment, habits, stress, health conditions)
entry_inputs.user_constraints: object (optional: budget_level, time_to_implement, physical_limitations, living_situation, flexibility_score)
entry_inputs.medical_context: string (optional: relevant medical history or medications)

terminal_outputs.problem_analysis: categorized root causes with severity and modifiability ranks
terminal_outputs.environmental_recommendations: array of 2-7 specific environmental changes with implementation details
terminal_outputs.behavioral_recommendations: array of 2-7 specific behavioral changes with sequencing and difficulty levels
terminal_outputs.implementation_protocol: phased rollout plan spanning 2-4+ weeks with success metrics and tracking
terminal_outputs.protocol_document: comprehensive 2500-3500 word formatted sleep hygiene guide ready for user

chain_definition.estimated_cost: 0 TRUST (single skill execution; no external dependencies)
chain_definition.execution_notes: This chain is a single-skill pipeline that executes all 7 phases of the Sleep Protocol Designer skill sequentially. Phases 4 and 5 (environmental and behavioral recommendations) can execute in parallel as they have independent inputs from Phase 3. The chain is deterministic: given the same sleep habits description and constraints, it produces consistent outputs.

coverage_report.goal: Analyze a user's sleep habits description and generate a personalized sleep hygiene protocol with specific environmental and behavioral changes.

goal_decomposition.requirement: Parse and structure user's sleep habits into a comprehensive profile
Assess sleep quality across multiple dimensions (onset latency, duration, continuity, daytime function, environment, consistency)
Identify root causes and contributing factors to sleep problems
Evaluate user constraints (budget, time, living situation, flexibility) and filter interventions by feasibility
Generate specific environmental recommendations (temperature, light, noise, bed quality, air quality)
Generate specific behavioral recommendations (caffeine, alcohol, exercise, screens, schedule, stress management)
Create phased implementation plan with timeline, daily actions, and success metrics
Produce comprehensive, user-ready protocol document with all sections (assessment, recommendations, timeline, tracking, troubleshooting)
Identify red flags requiring medical referral (insomnia >3 months, sleep apnea, narcolepsy, etc.)
Provide tracking templates and checkpoint schedule for progress monitoring

coverage_report.coverage_summary: All 10 goal requirements are fully covered by the chain. No gaps identified.
coverage_report.gap_analysis: No gaps. The Sleep Protocol Designer skill is self-contained and executes all phases required to achieve the stated goal.

execution_plan.title: Sleep Protocol Designer Chain - Execution Plan
execution_plan.description: Sequential execution of 7 phases within a single skill, with parallelization opportunities in phases 4-5.

execution_layers.phase: Phase 1: Sleep Profile Elicitation and Parsing
actions: Parse sleep habits description to extract explicit facts (sleep/wake times, duration, problems, environment, habits)
Identify implicit information (schedule regularity, stress levels, environmental quality)
Cross-reference with medical context if provided
Structure findings into sleep_profile object with confidence levels
Flag any missing critical information in clarification_flags
execution_layers.quality_gate: Every explicit statement appears in structured profile with confidence levels; at least 5 distinct data points present

execution_layers.phase: Phase 2: Sleep Quality Assessment and Problem Identification
actions: Score sleep quality across 6 dimensions (onset latency, duration adequacy, continuity, daytime function, environmental quality, behavioral consistency) using evidence-based rubric
Identify contributing factors for each dimension scoring <6
Categorize problems by root cause (environmental, behavioral, physiological, psychological)
Assign severity and modifiability ranks to each problem
Match problems to evidence-based interventions
Screen for red flags requiring medical referral
execution_layers.quality_gate: Every problem has specific cause traced to profile with evidence; at least one intervention per problem; red flags identified if present

actions: Extract or infer user constraints (budget, time, living situation, physical limitations, flexibility)
Assess feasibility of each intervention using formula: (Impact × 0.5) + (Ease × 0.3) + (Cost × 0.2)
Filter out interventions with feasibility score <5
Prioritize remaining interventions by feasibility score (highest first)
Identify constraints that fundamentally limit options
Flag if filtering eliminates all interventions for a problem
execution_layers.quality_gate: Every constraint explicitly stated or clearly inferred; feasibility scores calculated using formula; at least 3 feasible interventions remain

phase_4_actions: Filter interventions addressing environmental factors (temperature, light, noise, bed quality, air quality)
Generate specific, actionable environmental recommendations with rationale, implementation steps, cost, timeline, success indicators
Suggest workarounds for constrained environments (shared bedrooms, etc.)
phase_5_actions: Filter interventions addressing behavioral factors (caffeine, alcohol, exercise, screens, schedule, stress management)
Generate specific, actionable behavioral recommendations with rationale, implementation strategy, timeline, difficulty level, success indicators
Sequence changes by difficulty (easier first) and dependencies
Suggest replacement behaviors for eliminated habits
Include stress management and relaxation techniques if needed
execution_layers.quality_gate: All recommendations specific and measurable; cost/timeline realistic; no conflicts with user constraints; at least one quick win per category; success indicators measurable

execution_layers.phase: Phase 6: Implementation Protocol and Timeline Design
actions: Create phased rollout plan based on flexibility_score and time_to_implement (1-3 phases)
For each phase: specify recommendations, dates, daily/weekly actions, expected improvements
Ensure no phase has >5 simultaneous changes
Define success metrics (specific, measurable targets)
Create tracking template (daily sleep log with fields for time, duration, quality, awakenings, adherence, notes)
Schedule weekly checkpoints with review prompts
Create contingency plan with alternatives if recommendations not working after 2 weeks
execution_layers.quality_gate: No phase >5 changes; every recommendation appears in exactly one phase; metrics testable and measurable; tracking template simple (<2 min/day); at least 4 weekly checkpoints; contingencies for 50%+ of recommendations

actions: Compile comprehensive protocol document with 10 required sections: executive summary, sleep assessment, environmental changes, behavioral changes, 4-week implementation plan, daily tracking log, success metrics & checkpoints, troubleshooting guide, sleep science reference, personalization notes
Format for readability: clear headings, bullet points, tables, bold key terms
Use encouraging, non-judgmental tone with 'you' language
Ensure document is self-contained and actionable without external guidance
Add 'Next Steps' section at end
Target word count: 2500-3500 words
execution_layers.quality_gate: Readable by non-expert; every recommendation included with full context; actionable without external guidance; supportive tone; personalization notes reference user's constraints; red flags and medical referral guidance included; word count 2500-3500

execution_plan.parallelization_diagram: Layer 1 (Phase 1) → Layer 2 (Phase 2) → Layer 3 (Phase 3) → Layer 4 (Phases 4 & 5 PARALLEL) → Layer 5 (Phase 6) → Layer 6 (Phase 7)
execution_plan.parallelization_benefit: Phases 4 and 5 execute in parallel, saving ~12 minutes compared to sequential execution. Total time: 60 minutes (vs. 72 if fully sequential).
execution_plan.cost_estimate: 0 TRUST (single skill, no external dependencies, no credit costs)
assumptions: User provides sleep habits description of at least 100 characters with at least 3 required information types
User constraints are either provided or can be inferred from description
Medical context is optional; if provided, it is accurate and relevant
Skill executes all 7 phases in sequence; no early termination unless error handling is triggered
Output quality gates are met at each phase; if not, error handling adjusts the process

final_output.notes: The Sleep Protocol Designer is a comprehensive, self-contained skill that requires no additional skills to achieve the stated goal. The chain definition above represents the internal execution flow of this single skill across its 7 phases. In a multi-skill pipeline context, this skill could be preceded by a skill that gathers user input (e.g., 'Sleep Habits Questionnaire') and followed by a skill that implements tracking or provides ongoing support (e.g., 'Sleep Progress Monitor'). However, for the stated goal of 'analyze sleep habits and generate a personalized protocol,' this skill is complete and sufficient.
